                                              Top-line growth.
                                            </strong>
                                            Net sales grew from DKK 140,800m in 2021 to DKK 309,064m in 2025, a 2.2x increase in four years (CAGR ~21%). Year‑on‑year growth was particularly strong in 2022–2024, then moderated in 2025:
                                          </p>
                                          <ul>
                                            <li>
                                              <p>
                                                2021 → 2022: +10.9%
                                              </p>

                                              </p>
                                            </li>
                                            <li>
                                              <p>
                                                2023 → 2024: +31.3%
                                              </p>
                                            </li>
                                            <li>
                                              <p>
                                                2024 → 2025: +6.4%citeturn3view0
                                              </p>
                                            </li>
                                          </ul>
                                          <p>
                                            <strong>
                                              Operating profit.
                                            </strong>
                                            Operating profit more than doubled, from DKK 58,644m in 2021 to DKK 127,658m in 2025. Growth was strongest in 2023–2024 and then slightly negative in 2025:
                                          </p>
                                          <ul>
                                            <li>
                                              <p>
                                                2021 → 2022: +8.3%
                                              </p>
                                            </li>
                                            <li>
                                              <p>
                                                2022 → 2023: +27.6%
                                              </p>
                                            </li>
                                            <li>
                                              <p>
                                                2023 → 2024: +37.1%
                                              </p>
                                            </li>
                                            <li>
                                              <p>
                                                2024 → 2025: −0.5%citeturn3view0
                                              </p>
                                            </li>
                                          </ul>
                                          <p>
                                            <strong>
                                              Net profit.
                                            </strong>
                                            Net profit rose from DKK 47,757m in 2021 to DKK 102,434m in 2025, more than doubling over the period. Growth was extremely strong in 2023–2024, followed by a modest +1.4% in 2025.citeturn3view0
                                          </p>
                                          <p>
                                            <strong>
                                              EBITDA.
                                            </strong>
                                            EBITDA increased from DKK 64,669m in 2021 to DKK 149,640m in 2025, with particularly rapid expansion in 2023–2024 as new obesity/diabetes products scaled.citeturn3view0
                                          </p>

                                              </p>
                                            </li>

                                                2025 still shows revenue growth but at a much slower rate, and operating profit is slightly down despite higher sales, signalling rising cost pressure, capacity investments, and/or pricing headwinds.
                                              </p>
                                            </li>

                                            2. Profitability and Margins

                                            From the same five‑year overview, Novo Nordisk reports the following margins:citeturn3view0
                                          </p>

                                            <li>
                                              <p>
                                                Operating margin: 41.7% (2021) → 42.3% (2022) → 44.2% (2023) → 44.2% (2024) → 41.3% (2025)
                                              </p>
                                            </li>
                                            <li>
                                              <p>

                                          <p>
                                            <strong>
                                              Interpretation.
                                            </strong>
                                          </p>
                                          <ul>
                                            <li>
                                              <p>
                                                <strong>
                                                  Structural profitability is extraordinary.
                                                </strong>
                                                Operating margins in the low‑ to mid‑40s and net margins >30% place Novo Nordisk at the very high end of global large‑cap pharma.
                                              </p>
                                            </li>
                                            <li>
                                              <p>
                                                <strong>
                                                  2023–2024 were peak margin years.
                                                </strong>
                                                Margins benefited from strong pricing and scale leverage as GLP‑1 therapies ramped quickly.
                                              </p>
                                            </li>
                                            <li>
                                              <p>
                                                <strong>
                                                  2025 saw margin compression
                                                </strong>
                                                (operating margin down ~3 percentage points vs. 2024; net margin down ~1.7 points), reflecting increased R&D spend, manufacturing expansion, and intensifying competition—especially from rival GLP‑1 products.citeturn3view0turn1news23
                                              </p>
                                            </li>
                                          </ul>
                                          <hr />
                                          <h2 className="font-bold text-pretty [line-height:1.4] text-xl">
                                            3. Capital Intensity and Cash Generation
                                          </h2>
                                          <p>
                                            From the five‑year overview:citeturn3view0
                                          </p>
                                          <ul>
                                            <li>
                                              <p>
                                                <strong>
                                                  Capex and intangibles.
                                                </strong>
                                                Purchase of property, plant and equipment and intangible assets surged from DKK 7,385m in 2021 to DKK 90,113m in 2025, driven by large‑scale capacity expansions and acquisitions.
                                              </p>
                                            </li>
                                            <li>
                                              <p>
                                                <strong>
                                                  Free cash flow.
                                                </strong>
                                                Free cash flow was very strong and growing through 2023 (DKK 68,326m), turned sharply negative in 2024 (−14,707m) due to heavy investment and M&A, then rebounded to DKK 28,295m in 2025.
                                              </p>
                                            </li>
                                            <li>
                                              <p>
                                                <strong>
                                                  Balance sheet.
                                                </strong>
                                                Total assets more than doubled (DKK 194,508m → 542,902m), while equity rose from DKK 70,746m to DKK 194,047m. Net debt shifted from modest net cash in 2021 to substantial net cash again by 2024–2025 (negative net debt), indicating a very strong balance sheet.
                                              </p>
                                            </li>
                                          </ul>
                                          <p>
                                            <strong>
                                              Interpretation.
                                            </strong>
                                          </p>
                                          <ul>
                                            <li>
                                              <p>
                                                Novo Nordisk is aggressively investing in production capacity and pipeline assets to support long‑term GLP‑1 and obesity franchise growth.
                                              </p>
                                            </li>
                                            <li>
                                              <p>
                                                Despite heavy capex and acquisitions, the company continues to generate large free cash flow over the cycle and maintains net cash, which supports dividends and buybacks.
                                              </p>
                                            </li>
                                          </ul>
                                          <hr />
                                          <h2 className="font-bold text-pretty [line-height:1.4] text-xl">
                                            4. Shareholder Returns and Valuation Context
                                          </h2>
                                          <p>
                                            <strong>
                                              Dividends and buybacks.
                                            </strong>
                                          </p>
                                          <ul>
                                            <li>
                                              <p>
                                                Dividend per share increased from DKK 5.20 (2021) to DKK 11.70 (2025), and total dividend rose from DKK 23,711m to DKK 51,975m, with a payout ratio consistently around 50%.citeturn3view0
                                              </p>
                                            </li>
                                            <li>
                                              <p>
                                                Share repurchases peaked at nearly DKK 29,924m in 2023, then dropped sharply to DKK 1,388m in 2025 as the company prioritised investment and navigated a weaker share price.
                                              </p>
                                            </li>
                                          </ul>
                                          <p>
                                            <strong>
                                              Market context.
                                            </strong>
                                          </p>
                                          <ul>
                                            <li>
                                              <p>
                                                Novo Nordisk’s market capitalisation had become larger than Denmark’s GDP at its peak, making it Europe’s most valuable company, before a significant share price correction in 2025.citeturn1news23turn1search26
                                              </p>
                                            </li>
                                            <li>
                                              <p>
                                                Novo Holdings (the controlling shareholder) reported a large decline in assets under management in 2025, mainly due to the fall in Novo Nordisk’s market value, underscoring how central Novo Nordisk is to the broader Novo ecosystem.citeturn1search9
                                              </p>
                                            </li>
                                          </ul>
                                          <hr />

                                          </h2>
                                          <p>

                                          </p>

                                                <div className="scrollable-containerstyles__ScrollDelimiter-sc-15wt4a8-2 sc-kkdCOr fvVgds cDVQZp" />

                                                  <div className="table__TableContainer-sc-44znr6-0 eIBzeW table-container">

                                                              28.816162367468657

                                                <div className="scrollable-containerstyles__ScrollDelimiter-sc-15wt4a8-2 scrollable-container___StyledScrollDelimiter2-sc-1nkfsi9-0 fvVgds bMkjIm" />
                                              </div>
                                            </div>
                                          </div>
                                          <p />
                                          <hr />
                                          <h2 className="font-bold text-pretty [line-height:1.4] text-xl">
                                            6. Investment Takeaways (Non‑advice)
                                          </h2>
                                          <p>
                                            <strong>
                                              Strengths.
                                            </strong>
                                          </p>
                                          <ul>
                                            <li>
                                              <p>
                                                Exceptional revenue growth and very high, though slightly normalising, margins.
                                              </p>
                                            </li>
                                            <li>
                                              <p>
                                                Strong balance sheet with net cash and robust free cash flow over time.
                                              </p>
                                            </li>
                                            <li>
                                              <p>
                                                Structural tailwinds in obesity and diabetes care, with blockbuster GLP‑1 products.
                                              </p>
                                            </li>
                                          </ul>
                                          <p>
                                            <strong>
                                              Risks / watchpoints.
                                            </strong>
                                          </p>
                                          <ul>
                                            <li>
                                              <p>
                                                Slower growth and margin compression in 2025 suggest that the fastest phase of GLP‑1 ramp‑up may be past, while competition (notably from other GLP‑1 manufacturers and potential generics/compounded products) intensifies.citeturn1news17turn1news22
                                              </p>
                                            </li>
                                            <li>
                                              <p>
                                                Regulatory, pricing and reimbursement risks remain elevated given the scale and cost of obesity therapies.
                                              </p>
                                            </li>
                                            <li>
                                              <p>
                                                Heavy capex and acquisition spend raise execution risk on large manufacturing and pipeline projects.
                                              </p>
                                            </li>
                                          </ul>
                                          <p>
                                            <strong>
                                              Overall assessment.
                                            </strong>
                                            Novo Nordisk remains a highly profitable, innovation‑driven leader in metabolic disease. The 2021–2024 period delivered extraordinary growth and margin expansion; 2025 marks a transition to more moderate growth with higher investment and competitive pressure. Any financial model or valuation should therefore assume robust but decelerating growth, gradual margin normalisation from peak levels, and continued high capital expenditure in the medium term.
                                          </p>
                                          <p>
                                            <em>
                                              Note: This analysis is for informational purposes only and is not financial advice or a recommendation to buy or sell any security.
                                            </em>
                                          </p>
                                        </div>
                                      </div>
                                    </div>
                                  </div>
                                </div>
                              </div>
                            </div>
                            <div className="flex flex-1 flex-col gap-0! overflow-hidden">
                              <p className="text-foreground-primary font-bold! text-sm! leading-tight mb-0!">
                                Financial Analysis of Novo Nordisk with Graphs
                              </p>
                              <p className="text-foreground-secondary! text-sm! mb-0!">
                                Updated yesterday at 12:07
                              </p>
                              <p className="text-foreground-muted! text-sm! line-clamp-2 mb-0!">
                                Purpose. This document provides a concise financial analysis of Novo Nordisk based on the company’s five‑year overview from its 2025 Annual Report (figures in DKK million, 2021–2025).citeturn3view0 Scope. We focus on: Revenue and profit growth Profitability (margins) Capital intensity and cash generation Brief strategic/qualitative context --- Top-line growth. Net sales grew from DKK 140,800m in 2021 to DKK 309,064m in 2025, a 2.2x increase in four years (CAGR ~21%). Year‑on‑year growth was particularly strong in 2022–2024, then moderated in 2025: 2021 → 2022: +10.9% 2022 → 2023: +25.7% 2023 → 2024: +31.3% 2024 → 2025: +6.4%citeturn3view0 Operating profit. Operating profit more than doubled, from DKK 58,644m in 2021 to DKK 127,658m in 2025. Growth was strongest in 2023–2024 and then slightly negative in 2025: 2021 → 2022: +8.3% 2022 → 2023: +27.6% 2023 → 2024: +37.1% 2024 → 2025: −0.5%citeturn3view0 Net profit. Net profit rose from DKK 47,757m in 2021 to DKK 102,434m in 2025, more than doubling over the period. Growth was extremely strong in 2023–2024, followed by a modest +1.4% in 2025.citeturn3view0 EBITDA. EBITDA increased from DKK 64,669m in 2021 to DKK 149,640m in 2025, with particularly rapid expansion in 2023–2024 as new obesity/diabetes products scaled.citeturn3view0 <sana-plotly filename="novo_nordisk_profits_2021_2025.json" attachmentId="YWkZXGukA35q"></sana-plotly> Interpretation. Novo Nordisk delivered exceptional growth from 2021–2024, driven largely by GLP‑1 based obesity and diabetes products (e.g., Wegovy, Ozempic).citeturn1search4turn1search5 2025 still shows revenue growth but at a much slower rate, and operating profit is slightly down despite higher sales, signalling rising cost pressure, capacity investments, and/or pricing headwinds. --- From the same five‑year overview, Novo Nordisk reports the following margins:citeturn3view0 Operating margin: 41.7% (2021) → 42.3% (2022) → 44.2% (2023) → 44.2% (2024) → 41.3% (2025) Net profit margin: 33.9% (2021) → 31.4% (2022) → 36.0% (2023) → 34.8% (2024) → 33.1% (2025) <sana-plotly filename="novo_nordisk_margins_2021_2025.json" attachmentId="nwLhcFdd29oE"></sana-plotly> Interpretation. Structural profitability is extraordinary. Operating margins in the low‑ to mid‑40s and net margins >30% place Novo Nordisk at the very high end of global large‑cap pharma. 2023–2024 were peak margin years. Margins benefited from strong pricing and scale leverage as GLP‑1 therapies ramped quickly. 2025 saw margin compression (operating margin down ~3 percentage points vs. 2024; net margin down ~1.7 points), reflecting increased R&D spend, manufacturing expansion, and intensifying competition—especially from rival GLP‑1 products.citeturn3view0turn1news23 --- From the five‑year overview:citeturn3view0 Capex and intangibles. Purchase of property, plant and equipment and intangible assets surged from DKK 7,385m in 2021 to DKK 90,113m in 2025, driven by large‑scale capacity expansions and acquisitions. Free cash flow. Free cash flow was very strong and growing through 2023 (DKK 68,326m), turned sharply negative in 2024 (−14,707m) due to heavy investment and M&A, then rebounded to DKK 28,295m in 2025. Balance sheet. Total assets more than doubled (DKK 194,508m → 542,902m), while equity rose from DKK 70,746m to DKK 194,047m. Net debt shifted from modest net cash in 2021 to substantial net cash again by 2024–2025 (negative net debt), indicating a very strong balance sheet. Interpretation. Novo Nordisk is aggressively investing in production capacity and pipeline assets to support long‑term GLP‑1 and obesity franchise growth. Despite heavy capex and acquisitions, the company continues to generate large free cash flow over the cycle and maintains net cash, which supports dividends and buybacks. --- Dividends and buybacks. Dividend per share increased from DKK 5.20 (2021) to DKK 11.70 (2025), and total dividend rose from DKK 23,711m to DKK 51,975m, with a payout ratio consistently around 50%.citeturn3view0 Share repurchases peaked at nearly DKK 29,924m in 2023, then dropped sharply to DKK 1,388m in 2025 as the company prioritised investment and navigated a weaker share price. Market context. Novo Nordisk’s market capitalisation had become larger than Denmark’s GDP at its peak, making it Europe’s most valuable company, before a significant share price correction in 2025.citeturn1news23turn1search26 Novo Holdings (the controlling shareholder) reported a large decline in assets under management in 2025, mainly due to the fall in Novo Nordisk’s market value, underscoring how central Novo Nordisk is to the broader Novo ecosystem.citeturn1search9 --- The following table summarises the main figures used in this analysis. <sana-table filename="novo_nordisk_2021_2025_financials.csv" attachmentId="KueF6bGGWYdf"></sana-table> --- Strengths. Exceptional revenue growth and very high, though slightly normalising, margins. Strong balance sheet with net cash and robust free cash flow over time. Structural tailwinds in obesity and diabetes care, with blockbuster GLP‑1 products. Risks / watchpoints. Slower growth and margin compression in 2025 suggest that the fastest phase of GLP‑1 ramp‑up may be past, while competition (notably from other GLP‑1 manufacturers and potential generics/compounded products) intensifies.citeturn1news17turn1news22 Regulatory, pricing and reimbursement risks remain elevated given the scale and cost of obesity therapies. Heavy capex and acquisition spend raise execution risk on large manufacturing and pipeline projects. Overall assessment. Novo Nordisk remains a highly profitable, innovation‑driven leader in metabolic disease. The 2021–2024 period delivered extraordinary growth and margin expansion; 2025 marks a transition to more moderate growth with higher investment and competitive pressure. Any financial model or valuation should therefore assume robust but decelerating growth, gradual margin normalisation from peak levels, and continued high capital expenditure in the medium term. Note: This analysis is for informational purposes only and is not financial advice or a recommendation to buy or sell any security.
                              </p>
                            </div>
                          </div>
                        </div>
                        <div className="h-1" />
                        <div className="relative">
                          <div translate="no">

                                    </p>

                                          Year
                                        </p>

                                        <p>
                                          Net sales (DKK m)
                                        </p>

                                          Operating profit (DKK m)

                                <p>
                                  You can sort, filter, or export this table as needed.
                                </p>
                                <h3 className="font-bold text-pretty [line-height:1.4] text-lg">
                                  2. High‑level conclusions from the analysis
                                </h3>
                                <p>
                                  <strong>
                                    Growth.
                                  </strong>
                                  Novo Nordisk more than doubled net sales and net profit from 2021 to 2025, with exceptionally strong growth through 2023–2024, then a clear deceleration in 2025.
                                </p>
                                <p>
                                  <strong>
                                    Profitability.
                                  </strong>
                                  Operating margins in the low‑to‑mid‑40s and net margins above 30% reflect outstanding profitability, though both compressed in 2025 as investment and competitive pressure increased.
                                </p>
                                <p>
                                  <strong>
                                    Cash and balance sheet.
                                  </strong>
                                  Despite a year of heavily negative free cash flow in 2024 due to big capex and acquisitions, Novo Nordisk remains in a very solid net‑cash position with strong long‑term cash‑generation capacity.
                                </p>
                                <p>
                                  <strong>
                                    Investor angle (not advice).
                                  </strong>
                                  The company looks like a structurally strong, highly profitable leader in diabetes and obesity, transitioning from an explosive growth phase into a more mature but still attractive growth profile, with higher ongoing investment and more competition.
                                </p>
                                <hr />
                                <p>
                                  If you’d like, I can now:
                                </p>
                                <ul>
                                  <li>
                                    <p>
                                      Add a DCF-style projection section (with adjustable assumptions), or
                                    </p>
                                  </li>
                                  <li>
                                    <p>
                                      Extend the doc with peer comparison vs. Eli Lilly or other big pharma names.
                                    </p>
                                  </li>
                                </ul>

                                <div className="rounded-full flex justify-center items-center overflow-hidden relative p-1 transition-colors bg-foreground-5 group-hover:bg-foreground-8">

                              <div>
                                <button className="flex flex-row justify-center items-center text-sm leading-5 whitespace-nowrap transition-all duration-200 group button relative gap-1.5 cursor-pointer rounded-full font-bold border border-transparent disabled:cursor-default disabled:text-foreground-muted bg-transparent hover:not-disabled:bg-foreground-6 focus:bg-transparent active:bg-transparent disabled:bg-transparent w-[30px] h-[30px] p-0 text-foreground-80 hover:text-foreground-primary" aria-label="Copy">
                                  <div className="sc-fYIFVK lnlxNv">
                                    <div className="copy-to-clipboard-button___StyledMotionDiv2-sc-1bkcun8-0 homAij">
                                      <svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="16" height="16" fill="none" />
                                    </div>
                                  </div>
                                </button>
                              </div>
                              <div>
                                <button className="flex flex-row justify-center items-center text-sm leading-5 whitespace-nowrap text-foreground-primary transition-all duration-200 group button relative gap-1.5 cursor-pointer rounded-full font-bold border border-transparent disabled:cursor-default disabled:text-foreground-muted bg-transparent hover:not-disabled:bg-foreground-6 focus:bg-transparent active:bg-transparent disabled:bg-transparent w-[30px] h-[30px] p-0 chatstyles__MessageActionButton-sc-1kclmzs-0 byljna" aria-label="Upvote">
                                  <div>
                                    <svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="16" height="16" fill="none" viewBox="0 0 16 16" color="currentColor" />

                              </div>
                              <div>
                                <button className="flex flex-row justify-center items-center text-sm leading-5 whitespace-nowrap text-foreground-primary transition-all duration-200 group button relative gap-1.5 cursor-pointer rounded-full font-bold border border-transparent disabled:cursor-default disabled:text-foreground-muted bg-transparent hover:not-disabled:bg-foreground-6 focus:bg-transparent active:bg-transparent disabled:bg-transparent w-[30px] h-[30px] p-0 chatstyles__MessageActionButton-sc-1kclmzs-0 byljna" aria-label="Downvote">

                      <div className="flex-1 flex flex-col items-center justify-center px-6 pb-8 pt-16">
                        <div className="@container w-full relative bg-paper rounded-2xl border-[1px] border-background-primary dark:border-[rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.01)] max-w-[820px] @[1200px]:max-w-[1000px] @[1600px]:max-w-[1200px]">
                          <div className="p-8 pt-7 w-full h-full @[600px]:p-15 @[600px]:pt-14">
                            <div className="flex flex-col h-full min-h-screen">
                              <div tabIndex="0" aria-label="Edit Financial Analysis of Novo Nordisk with Graphs" className="mx-auto w-full overscroll-none flex-1 max-w-[1000px] -mt-5">
                                <div />
                                <div>
                                  <input type="file" accept="image/*" className="hidden" />
                                </div>
                                <div>
                                  <div>
                                    <div contenteditable="true" translate="no" className="tiptap ProseMirror focus:outline-none" tabIndex="0">
                                      <h2>
                                        Overview
                                      </h2>
                                      <p>
                                        <strong>
                                          Purpose.
                                        </strong>
                                        This document provides a concise financial analysis of Novo Nordisk based on the company’s five‑year overview from its 2025 Annual Report (figures in DKK million, 2021–2025).citeturn3view0
                                      </p>
                                      <p>
                                        <strong>
                                          Scope.
                                        </strong>
                                        We focus on:
                                      </p>
                                      <ul className="tight" data-tight="true">
                                        <li>
                                          <p>
                                            Revenue and profit growth
                                          </p>
                                        </li>
                                        <li>
                                          <p>
                                            Profitability (margins)
                                          </p>
                                        </li>
                                        <li>
                                          <p>
                                            Capital intensity and cash generation
                                          </p>
                                        </li>
                                        <li>
                                          <p>
                                            Brief strategic/qualitative context
                                          </p>
                                        </li>
                                      </ul>
                                      <hr contenteditable="false" />
                                      <h2>
                                        1. Revenue and Profit Trends (2021–2025)
                                      </h2>
                                      <p>
                                        <strong>
                                          Top-line growth.
                                        </strong>
                                        Net sales grew from DKK 140,800m in 2021 to DKK 309,064m in 2025, a 2.2x increase in four years (CAGR ~21%). Year‑on‑year growth was particularly strong in 2022–2024, then moderated in 2025:
                                      </p>
                                      <ul className="tight" data-tight="true">
                                        <li>
                                          <p>
                                            2021 → 2022: +10.9%
                                          </p>
                                        </li>
                                        <li>
                                          <p>
                                            2022 → 2023: +25.7%
                                          </p>
                                        </li>
                                        <li>
                                          <p>
                                            2023 → 2024: +31.3%
                                          </p>
                                        </li>
                                        <li>
                                          <p>
                                            2024 → 2025: +6.4%citeturn3view0
                                          </p>
                                        </li>
                                      </ul>
                                      <p>
                                        <strong>
                                          Operating profit.
                                        </strong>
                                        Operating profit more than doubled, from DKK 58,644m in 2021 to DKK 127,658m in 2025. Growth was strongest in 2023–2024 and then slightly negative in 2025:
                                      </p>
                                      <ul className="tight" data-tight="true">
                                        <li>
                                          <p>
                                            2021 → 2022: +8.3%
                                          </p>
                                        </li>
                                        <li>
                                          <p>
                                            2022 → 2023: +27.6%
                                          </p>
                                        </li>
                                        <li>
                                          <p>
                                            2023 → 2024: +37.1%
                                          </p>
                                        </li>
                                        <li>
                                          <p>
                                            2024 → 2025: −0.5%citeturn3view0
                                          </p>
                                        </li>
                                      </ul>
                                      <p>
                                        <strong>
                                          Net profit.
                                        </strong>
                                        Net profit rose from DKK 47,757m in 2021 to DKK 102,434m in 2025, more than doubling over the period. Growth was extremely strong in 2023–2024, followed by a modest +1.4% in 2025.citeturn3view0
                                      </p>
                                      <p>
                                        <strong>
                                          EBITDA.
                                        </strong>
                                        EBITDA increased from DKK 64,669m in 2021 to DKK 149,640m in 2025, with particularly rapid expansion in 2023–2024 as new obesity/diabetes products scaled.citeturn3view0
                                      </p>
                                      <h3>
                                        Visual: Revenue and Profit Development
                                      </h3>
                                      <div className="react-renderer node-sana-plotly" contenteditable="false">
                                        <div as="div" contenteditable="false" data-inline-citation="true" className="cursor-pointer" data-node-view-wrapper="">
                                          <div>
                                            <div className="plotly-styled-components__StyledPlotly-sc-qv5rwp-0 cQgcnT">
                                              <div className="plotly-renderer___StyledFadeInOut-sc-1f23x0g-0 fTVMva">
                                                <div className="plotly-styled-components__StyledPlot-sc-qv5rwp-1 gZevSI js-plotly-plot">
                                                  <div className="plot-container plotly">
                                                    <div className="user-select-none svg-container">
                                                      <svg className="main-svg" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink" width="697.333" height="523" />
                                                      <div className="gl-container" />
                                                      <svg className="main-svg" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink" width="697.333" height="523" />
                                                      <div className="modebar-container">
                                                        <div id="modebar-e8f5d8" className="modebar modebar--hover ease-bg">
                                                          <div className="modebar-group" />
                                                          <div className="modebar-group" />
                                                          <div className="modebar-group" />
                                                          <div className="modebar-group">
                                                            <a rel="tooltip" className="modebar-btn" data-title="Zoom in on plot" data-toggle="false" data-gravity="n" data-btn-style-event-added="true">
                                                              <svg viewBox="0 0 16 16" className="icon" height="1em" width="1em" />
                                                            </a>
                                                            <a rel="tooltip" className="modebar-btn" data-title="Reset axes to default" data-toggle="false" data-gravity="n" data-btn-style-event-added="true">
                                                              <svg viewBox="0 0 16 16" className="icon" height="1em" width="1em" />
                                                            </a>
                                                            <a rel="tooltip" className="modebar-btn" data-title="Download plot as a png" data-toggle="false" data-gravity="n" data-btn-style-event-added="true">
                                                              <svg viewBox="0 0 16 16" className="icon" height="1em" width="1em" />
                                                            </a>
                                                            <a rel="tooltip" className="modebar-btn" data-title="Copy plot as SVG to clipboard" data-toggle="false" data-gravity="n" data-btn-style-event-added="true">
                                                              <svg viewBox="0 0 16 16" className="icon" height="1em" width="1em" />
                                                            </a>
                                                          </div>
                                                        </div>
                                                      </div>
                                                      <svg className="main-svg" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink" width="697.333" height="523" />
                                                    </div>
                                                  </div>
                                                </div>
                                              </div>
                                            </div>
                                          </div>
                                        </div>
                                      </div>
                                      <p>
                                        <strong>
                                          Interpretation.
                                        </strong>
                                      </p>
                                      <ul className="tight" data-tight="true">
                                        <li>
                                          <p>
                                            Novo Nordisk delivered exceptional growth from 2021–2024, driven largely by GLP‑1 based obesity and diabetes products (e.g., Wegovy, Ozempic).citeturn1search4turn1search5
                                          </p>
                                        </li>
                                        <li>
                                          <p>
                                            2025 still shows revenue growth but at a much slower rate, and operating profit is slightly down despite higher sales, signalling rising cost pressure, capacity investments, and/or pricing headwinds.
                                          </p>
                                        </li>
                                      </ul>
                                      <hr contenteditable="false" />
                                      <h2>
                                        2. Profitability and Margins
                                      </h2>
                                      <p>
                                        From the same five‑year overview, Novo Nordisk reports the following margins:citeturn3view0
                                      </p>
                                      <ul className="tight" data-tight="true">
                                        <li>
                                          <p>
                                            Operating margin: 41.7% (2021) → 42.3% (2022) → 44.2% (2023) → 44.2% (2024) → 41.3% (2025)
                                          </p>
                                        </li>
                                        <li>
                                          <p>
                                            Net profit margin: 33.9% (2021) → 31.4% (2022) → 36.0% (2023) → 34.8% (2024) → 33.1% (2025)
                                          </p>
                                        </li>
                                      </ul>
                                      <h3>
                                        Visual: Margin Trends
                                      </h3>
                                      <div className="react-renderer node-sana-plotly" contenteditable="false">
                                        <div as="div" contenteditable="false" data-inline-citation="true" className="cursor-pointer" data-node-view-wrapper="">
                                          <div>
                                            <div className="plotly-styled-components__StyledPlotly-sc-qv5rwp-0 cQgcnT">
                                              <div className="plotly-renderer___StyledFadeInOut-sc-1f23x0g-0 fTVMva">
                                                <div className="plotly-styled-components__StyledPlot-sc-qv5rwp-1 gZevSI js-plotly-plot">
                                                  <div className="plot-container plotly">
                                                    <div className="user-select-none svg-container">
                                                      <svg className="main-svg" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink" width="697.333" height="523" />
                                                      <div className="gl-container" />
                                                      <svg className="main-svg" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink" width="697.333" height="523" />
                                                      <div className="modebar-container">
                                                        <div id="modebar-8b61de" className="modebar modebar--hover ease-bg">
                                                          <div className="modebar-group" />
                                                          <div className="modebar-group" />
                                                          <div className="modebar-group" />
                                                          <div className="modebar-group">
                                                            <a rel="tooltip" className="modebar-btn" data-title="Zoom in on plot" data-toggle="false" data-gravity="n" data-btn-style-event-added="true">
                                                              <svg viewBox="0 0 16 16" className="icon" height="1em" width="1em" />
                                                            </a>
                                                            <a rel="tooltip" className="modebar-btn" data-title="Reset axes to default" data-toggle="false" data-gravity="n" data-btn-style-event-added="true">
                                                              <svg viewBox="0 0 16 16" className="icon" height="1em" width="1em" />
                                                            </a>
                                                            <a rel="tooltip" className="modebar-btn" data-title="Download plot as a png" data-toggle="false" data-gravity="n" data-btn-style-event-added="true">
                                                              <svg viewBox="0 0 16 16" className="icon" height="1em" width="1em" />
                                                            </a>
                                                            <a rel="tooltip" className="modebar-btn" data-title="Copy plot as SVG to clipboard" data-toggle="false" data-gravity="n" data-btn-style-event-added="true">
                                                              <svg viewBox="0 0 16 16" className="icon" height="1em" width="1em" />
                                                            </a>
                                                          </div>
                                                        </div>
                                                      </div>
                                                      <svg className="main-svg" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink" width="697.333" height="523" />
                                                    </div>
                                                  </div>
                                                </div>
                                              </div>
                                            </div>
                                          </div>
                                        </div>
                                      </div>
                                      <p>
                                        <strong>
                                          Interpretation.
                                        </strong>
                                      </p>
                                      <ul className="tight" data-tight="true">
                                        <li>
                                          <p>
                                            <strong>
                                              Structural profitability is extraordinary.
                                            </strong>
                                            Operating margins in the low‑ to mid‑40s and net margins >30% place Novo Nordisk at the very high end of global large‑cap pharma.
                                          </p>
                                        </li>
                                        <li>
                                          <p>
                                            <strong>
                                              2023–2024 were peak margin years.
                                            </strong>
                                            Margins benefited from strong pricing and scale leverage as GLP‑1 therapies ramped quickly.
                                          </p>
                                        </li>
                                        <li>
                                          <p>
                                            <strong>
                                              2025 saw margin compression
                                            </strong>
                                            (operating margin down ~3 percentage points vs. 2024; net margin down ~1.7 points), reflecting increased R&D spend, manufacturing expansion, and intensifying competition—especially from rival GLP‑1 products.citeturn3view0turn1news23
                                          </p>
                                        </li>
                                      </ul>
                                      <hr contenteditable="false" />
                                      <h2>
                                        3. Capital Intensity and Cash Generation
                                      </h2>
                                      <p>
                                        From the five‑year overview:citeturn3view0
                                      </p>
                                      <ul className="tight" data-tight="true">
                                        <li>
                                          <p>
                                            <strong>
                                              Capex and intangibles.
                                            </strong>
                                            Purchase of property, plant and equipment and intangible assets surged from DKK 7,385m in 2021 to DKK 90,113m in 2025, driven by large‑scale capacity expansions and acquisitions.
                                          </p>
                                        </li>
                                        <li>
                                          <p>
                                            <strong>
                                              Free cash flow.
                                            </strong>
                                            Free cash flow was very strong and growing through 2023 (DKK 68,326m), turned sharply negative in 2024 (−14,707m) due to heavy investment and M&A, then rebounded to DKK 28,295m in 2025.
                                          </p>
                                        </li>
                                        <li>
                                          <p>
                                            <strong>
                                              Balance sheet.
                                            </strong>
                                            Total assets more than doubled (DKK 194,508m → 542,902m), while equity rose from DKK 70,746m to DKK 194,047m. Net debt shifted from modest net cash in 2021 to substantial net cash again by 2024–2025 (negative net debt), indicating a very strong balance sheet.
                                          </p>
                                        </li>
                                      </ul>
                                      <p>
                                        <strong>
                                          Interpretation.
                                        </strong>
                                      </p>
                                      <ul className="tight" data-tight="true">
                                        <li>
                                          <p>
                                            Novo Nordisk is aggressively investing in production capacity and pipeline assets to support long‑term GLP‑1 and obesity franchise growth.
                                          </p>
                                        </li>
                                        <li>
                                          <p>
                                            Despite heavy capex and acquisitions, the company continues to generate large free cash flow over the cycle and maintains net cash, which supports dividends and buybacks.
                                          </p>
                                        </li>
                                      </ul>
                                      <hr contenteditable="false" />
                                      <h2>
                                        4. Shareholder Returns and Valuation Context
                                      </h2>
                                      <p>
                                        <strong>
                                          Dividends and buybacks.
                                        </strong>
                                      </p>
                                      <ul className="tight" data-tight="true">
                                        <li>
                                          <p>
                                            Dividend per share increased from DKK 5.20 (2021) to DKK 11.70 (2025), and total dividend rose from DKK 23,711m to DKK 51,975m, with a payout ratio consistently around 50%.citeturn3view0
                                          </p>
                                        </li>
                                        <li>
                                          <p>
                                            Share repurchases peaked at nearly DKK 29,924m in 2023, then dropped sharply to DKK 1,388m in 2025 as the company prioritised investment and navigated a weaker share price.
                                          </p>
                                        </li>
                                      </ul>
                                      <p>
                                        <strong>
                                          Market context.
                                        </strong>
                                      </p>
                                      <ul className="tight" data-tight="true">
                                        <li>
                                          <p>
                                            Novo Nordisk’s market capitalisation had become larger than Denmark’s GDP at its peak, making it Europe’s most valuable company, before a significant share price correction in 2025.citeturn1news23turn1search26
                                          </p>
                                        </li>
                                        <li>
                                          <p>
                                            Novo Holdings (the controlling shareholder) reported a large decline in assets under management in 2025, mainly due to the fall in Novo Nordisk’s market value, underscoring how central Novo Nordisk is to the broader Novo ecosystem.citeturn1search9
                                          </p>
                                        </li>
                                      </ul>
                                      <hr contenteditable="false" />
                                      <h2>
                                        5. Data Table (2021–2025 Key Figures)
                                      </h2>
                                      <p>
                                        The following table summarises the main figures used in this analysis.
                                      </p>
                                      <p>
                                        <br className="ProseMirror-trailingBreak" />
                                      </p>
                                      <hr contenteditable="false" />
                                      <h2>
                                        6. Investment Takeaways (Non‑advice)
                                      </h2>
                                      <p>
                                        <strong>
                                          Strengths.
                                        </strong>
                                      </p>
                                      <ul className="tight" data-tight="true">
                                        <li>
                                          <p>
                                            Exceptional revenue growth and very high, though slightly normalising, margins.
                                          </p>
                                        </li>
                                        <li>
                                          <p>
                                            Strong balance sheet with net cash and robust free cash flow over time.
                                          </p>
                                        </li>
                                        <li>
                                          <p>
                                            Structural tailwinds in obesity and diabetes care, with blockbuster GLP‑1 products.
                                          </p>
                                        </li>
                                      </ul>
                                      <p>
                                        <strong>
                                          Risks / watchpoints.
                                        </strong>
                                      </p>
                                      <ul className="tight" data-tight="true">
                                        <li>
                                          <p>
                                            Slower growth and margin compression in 2025 suggest that the fastest phase of GLP‑1 ramp‑up may be past, while competition (notably from other GLP‑1 manufacturers and potential generics/compounded products) intensifies.citeturn1news17turn1news22
                                          </p>
                                        </li>
                                        <li>
                                          <p>
                                            Regulatory, pricing and reimbursement risks remain elevated given the scale and cost of obesity therapies.
                                          </p>
                                        </li>
                                        <li>
                                          <p>
                                            Heavy capex and acquisition spend raise execution risk on large manufacturing and pipeline projects.
                                          </p>
                                        </li>
                                      </ul>
                                      <p>
                                        <strong>
                                          Overall assessment.
                                        </strong>
                                        Novo Nordisk remains a highly profitable, innovation‑driven leader in metabolic disease. The 2021–2024 period delivered extraordinary growth and margin expansion; 2025 marks a transition to more moderate growth with higher investment and competitive pressure. Any financial model or valuation should therefore assume robust but decelerating growth, gradual margin normalisation from peak levels, and continued high capital expenditure in the medium term.
                                      </p>
                                      <p>
                                        <em>
                                          Note: This analysis is for informational purposes only and is not financial advice or a recommendation to buy or sell any security.
                                        </em>
                                      </p>
                                    </div>
                                  </div>
                                </div>
                              </div>
                            </div>
                          </div>
                        </div>
                      </div>
                    </div>
                  </div>
                </div>
              </div>
              <div className="absolute top-0 bottom-0 w-6 h-full cursor-col-resize touch-none -left-2 before:content-[&quot;&quot;] before:absolute before:top-0 before:bottom-0 before:left-2 before:w-px before:bg-foreground-6 hover:before:bg-foreground-20 active:before:bg-foreground-20 transition-colors user-select-none" role="separator" aria-orientation="vertical" aria-label="Resize canvas sidebar" />
            </div>
          </aside>
          <aside data-right-sidebar="true" className="right-sidebarstyles__StyledRightSidebar-sc-u1gedy-0 loUNec">
            <button className="flex flex-row justify-center items-center text-sm leading-5 whitespace-nowrap duration-200 group button gap-1.5 cursor-pointer rounded-full font-bold border border-transparent disabled:cursor-default disabled:text-foreground-muted bg-transparent hover:not-disabled:bg-foreground-6 disabled:bg-transparent w-9 h-9 p-0 z-13 absolute text-foreground-secondary hover:text-foreground-primary transition hover:bg-foreground-5 active:bg-foreground-5 focus:bg-foreground-5 top-2.5 md:top-3 right-3 opacity-0 pointer-events-none">
              <div>
                <svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="16" height="16" fill="none" viewBox="0 0 16 16" color="currentColor" />
              </div>
            </button>
          </aside>
        </section>
      </main>
    </div>
  );
}
